meta_title: For Students: debugging with AI to Solve slow development speed (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome slow development speed. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Debugging with AI to Solve Slow Development
content.seo_description: Learn how to debug with AI and overcome slow development speed. A beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your coding workflow.
content.answer_block: Stop staring at red error logs for hours. By integrating AI-assisted debugging through Antigravity and Claude Code, you transform your workflow from manual trial-and-error to rapid, automated resolution. This approach identifies root causes instantly, allowing you to focus on building features rather than wrestling with syntax and logic bugs.
content.intro:
The cursor blinks in the silence of your code editor, mocking the hours you have spent chasing a single undefined variable. As a student developer, your greatest enemy is not a lack of intelligence, but the friction of slow development speed that turns a weekend project into a month-long slog.

But what if you could compress that frustration into seconds? By leveraging the raw power of AI-native debugging tools, you stop being a code detective and start being an architect. This guide will show you how to weaponize Antigravity and Claude Code to clear roadblocks and ship your first real-world application with professional-grade speed.

steps.title: Contextualize the Error
steps.description: Before fixing, you must define the scope. Feed the AI the specific error stack trace alongside your current file structure to give it the necessary context.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the following stack trace and the current file snippet. Identify the file and line number causing the crash and explain the logical mismatch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude read src/main.py --instruction 'Explain why this file is throwing a runtime error.'

steps.title: Automated Root Cause Analysis
steps.description: Use the AI to simulate potential fixes without altering your codebase yet. This ensures you understand the 'why' before applying the 'how'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Suggest three potential root causes for this behavior and rank them by likelihood. Do not write code yet.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'What is the most likely cause for this specific TypeError given the function inputs?'

steps.title: Iterative Patching
steps.description: Apply the fix in small, testable chunks. Always verify the fix with a unit test to prevent regression.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a refactored version of the function that handles this edge case safely. Include a comment explaining the fix.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit src/utils.py --instruction 'Implement a null check for the user_id variable.'

steps.title: Validation and Testing
steps.description: Verify your fix works in the local environment and ensure no side effects were introduced to adjacent modules.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a pytest suite for this function that covers the edge cases we just addressed.

steps.title: Documentation and Learning
steps.description: Document the bug fix in your project README to solidify your knowledge and build a professional portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Summarize this bug and the solution into a professional commit message and a brief 'Lessons Learned' snippet for my documentation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit -m 'Fix: resolve runtime error in user authentication logic'

pro_tips: Always ask the AI to explain the 'why' behind a fix; if you don't understand the solution, you haven't actually leveled up.
Use Claude Code to recursively scan your entire directory for similar patterns if a bug appears in one place, it is likely lurking elsewhere.
Set your AI tool's temperature to low (0.1-0.2) when debugging to force deterministic, logic-heavy responses instead of creative guessing.
Treat your AI assistant as a pair programmer: explain your thought process out loud to the AI to help it catch your logical fallacies faster.
Create a 'debug-log.md' file where you force the AI to record the nature of the bug, the fix, and the prevention strategy for future reference.
resources: Best Practices for Prompt Engineering: https://learnprompting.org
content.next_steps: Once you have successfully debugged your current project, try refactoring a legacy function you wrote last month. Compare the time saved using AI versus your old manual method, then commit your code to GitHub to showcase your progress.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting slow development hold your dreams hostage. You have the tools, the strategy, and the roadmap to ship faster than ever before. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
